title: Morse Code Sender
subtitle: Blink "SOS" in Morse code and print it to Serial.

hints.content: Morse "S" is three short blinks, "O" is three long blinks.
Try a short blink = 200ms on, long blink = 600ms on, with a short gap between blinks.
Write a blink(int ms) helper that does digitalWrite HIGH, delay(ms), digitalWrite LOW, delay(200) — call it 3x short, 3x long, 3x short, then Serial.println("SOS").

narrative.briefing: Before radio voice transmission, ships sent emergency calls in Morse code — dots and dashes encoded as short and long signals. Recreate the most famous Morse message of all: SOS.
narrative.context: Morse code is just timing: a "dot" is a short pulse, a "dash" is roughly three times as long. The same idea shows up everywhere in electronics, from PWM to data encoding.
narrative.successMessage: S-O-S received! You just sent a real distress signal using nothing but timing.
